items.q: What is the scope of a local variable?
items.correct: Accessible only within the function/block where it is declared.
items.random_wrong: Stored on a local computer rather than a server.
items.why: Students confuse 'local' with 'on this computer'. In programming, scope refers to the CODE REGION where the variable name resolves - local variables vanish when the function returns.

items.q: What is the denary value of binary 10110?
items.why: Students forget to apply place values. Each position is a power of 2 from right (1, 2, 4, 8, 16...). Reading binary as denary is the most common error.

items.q: What happens when 11111111 + 1 is computed in an 8-bit register?
items.correct: Overflow - the result requires 9 bits but only 8 are available.
items.why: Students miss that registers have fixed width. The mathematical result is 9 bits, but the 8-bit register drops the carry-out, producing 00000000 with an overflow flag.

items.q: SQL is best described as which paradigm?
items.why: Correct answer: Declarative. “Imperative” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which paradigm describes step-by-step state changes?
items.why: Correct answer: Imperative. “Declarative” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many of the following are imperative: C, SQL, HTML, Python? Count only imperative.
items.why: The real figure is 2. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
